meta.title: Text
meta.description: Text styles are predefined collections of typography rules (including font-size, font-weight, line-height, and letter-spacing) that create reusable, consistent styles.

mdx:
## Overview

Readable and consistent text styling is important to creating effective user
friendly and efficient products. These styles and tools will help give the
building blocks and provide more options for creation, and there are still ways
to allow for customization for the rare cases it is needed.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=6041-23913&m=dev)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Typography scale and hierarchy

Text styles here are recommended for most body styled text, and made to fit well
with component sizes. If you need to render large, bold text with a semantic
meaning, you should use the heading component instead.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" textStyle="md">Paragraph md</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Primary body text, long descriptions, standard content blocks.</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" textStyle="sm">Paragraph sm</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Secondary text, metadata, table content.</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" textStyle="xs">Paragraph xs</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Tertiary content, small notes, timestamps.</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" textStyle="caption">CAPTION</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Used for specialized, tracked metadata. (Includes letter-spacing: 1px).</Text>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Text tones and semantic coloring

Text tones use color to semantically convey status, intent, or hierarchy. The
default tone is used for the majority of UI text.

**Color Rule:** All colored font-color is always set to step 11 of the given
color scale (e.g., tomato.11, indigo.11). This ensures high contrast and
accessibility.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" color="fg">Default tone</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Standard body copy, primary content.</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" color="fg.subtle">Secondary</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Helper text, metadata, inactive or less important UI elements.</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" color="primary.11">Primary</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Mainly used for links or branded messages.</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" color="info.11">Info</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Non-critical feedback, system messages.</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" color="positive.11">Positive</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Successful actions, positive status indicators (e.g., "Complete," "Saved").</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" color="critical.11">Critical</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Validation errors, destructive warnings, critical status indicators.</Text>
    </Box>
    <Box>
      <Text as="span" color="warning.11">Warning</Text>
      <Text as="span"> - Warnings, temporary issues, actions requiring review.</Text>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
    <Text as="h2" fontSize="600" fontWeight="700">Header text</Text>
    <Text>Body text</Text>
    <Text textTransform="uppercase" letterSpacing="1px" fontSize="350">CAPTIONS</Text>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Contrast:** The explicit rule of using color step 11 for colored text
  ensures that colored text meets or exceeds minimum WCAG contrast ratios
  against light backgrounds.
- **Semantic HTML:** Ensure that text styles map correctly to semantic HTML tags
  (e.g., Headlines should use `<h1>` through `<h6>`).
- **Truncation:** For long body copy or component-specific labels, define clear
  rules for truncation (using ellipses) and ensure a tooltip reveals the full
  text on hover/focus.
- **Avoid overlap:** Never overlap text elements or place complex colored
  backgrounds behind text, as this voids the consistent contrast guaranteed by
  the defined color scale.
- **Scale usage:** Only use component-specific styles (e.g., button md) for
  their intended purpose; do not use them to style arbitrary body text.

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

The purpose of these guidelines is to establish a clear typographic hierarchy,
optimize content readability, and enforce accessibility standards.

### Best practices

- **Keep hierarchy consistent:** Use a limited, predefined set of text styles to
  maintain visual consistency across all products. Add custom styles with
  caution.
- **Prioritize line length:** Use optimal line lengths (typically 50 to 75
  characters per line) to prevent eye strain and facilitate reading.
- **Use concise language:** Write clearly, avoiding unnecessary jargon or overly
  long sentences to reduce cognitive load.
- **Break up content:** Use short paragraphs, lists, and headings to break up
  large blocks of text, making content easier to scan.
- **Maintain consistent tone:** The language, style, and formality should be
  consistent throughout the application, reflecting the brand's voice.
- **Handle truncation gracefully:** When necessary, truncate long text with
  ellipses and provide a tooltip or interaction element to reveal the full
  content.
- **Keep length in mind for translation:** When supporting text localization,
  length can expand more than 20%. Design experiences that can be flexible to
  shortening and lengthening of content.

### Text Do's and Don'ts

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Use predefined headings, body sizes, and weights to clearly signal the
>   importance of content.
> - Always maintain sufficient contrast between text and its background to meet
>   WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 minimum).
> - Keep line length between 50 to 75 characters to maximize reading speed and
>   comfort.
> - Ensure adequate vertical spacing (line-height) to prevent text lines from
>   colliding and improve readability.
> - Write clearly, avoiding unnecessary jargon and overly long sentences.
> - Utilize short paragraphs, bullet points, and headings to make content
>   scannable.
> - Ensure any text used within images or icons has a text alternative or
>   aria-label for screen readers.

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Avoid introducing too many different font sizes, weights, or families, which
>   destroys visual consistency.
> - Never use color as the only method to convey meaning (e.g., indicating an
>   error solely with red text).
> - Avoid excessively long lines of text (over 80 characters), which makes
>   tracking difficult and causes fatigue.
> - Avoid line heights that are too small, which makes text visually dense and
>   difficult to read.
> - Never truncate text without providing a clear way (like a tooltip) to view
>   the full content.
> - Avoid pairing text colors that barely distinguish themselves from the
>   background, even if they look "stylish."
> - Stick to the approved typeface(s) in your design system.
